LEGACY-PRENCT-ACCENT-1.name: ACCENT-1
LEGACY-PRENCT-ACCENT-1.group: Infliximab 5 mg/kg IV (0, 2, 6 wk) | TNFi (moderate-severe CD; vs Placebo; ACCENT-1 wk 2 response + wk 30 remission among responders)

allOutcomes.title: Clinical remission (CDAI <150) at wk 30 among wk-2 responders infliximab vs placebo (ACCENT-1; induction subset)

LEGACY-PRENCT-ACCENT-1.snippet: Hanauer SB et al. Lancet 2002;359:1541-1549 (ACCENT-1). First biologic in CD. Induction+maintenance design.

LEGACY-PRENCT-CLASSIC-1.name: CLASSIC-1
LEGACY-PRENCT-CLASSIC-1.group: Adalimumab 160/80 mg SC induction | TNFi (moderate-severe CD; vs Placebo; CLASSIC-1 wk 4 remission)

allOutcomes.title: Clinical remission at wk 4 adalimumab 160/80 vs placebo (CLASSIC-1 primary; CDAI <150)

LEGACY-PRENCT-CLASSIC-1.snippet: Hanauer SB et al. Gastroenterology 2006;130:323-333 (CLASSIC-1). Adalimumab induction in TNF-naive CD.

NCT00152490.name: PRECiSE-1
NCT00152490.group: Certolizumab 400 mg SC (wk 0, 2, 4) | TNFi (moderate-severe CD; vs Placebo; PRECiSE-1 wk 6 response)

allOutcomes.title: Clinical response (CDAI decrease >=100) at wk 6 certolizumab vs placebo (PRECiSE-1 primary)

NCT00152490.snippet: Sandborn WJ et al. NEJM 2007;357:228-238 (PRECiSE-1). Pegylated anti-TNF; response primary (not remission).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Definitive diagnosis of Crohn's disease confirmed (at least 3 months prior to study entry) either by radiological, endoscopic or histological evidence, affecting the terminal ileum (L1), colon (L2) or ileocolon (L3*. *Vienna Classification (1998)~* Active Crohn's disease (≥ 220 and ≤ 450) scored over the 7 days prior to the first dose of study drug.~* Male and female aged 18 years or above at screening.~* Patients who meet all concomitant medication criteria in the proto...

NCT01224171.name: GEMINI-2
NCT01224171.group: Vedolizumab 300 mg IV induction (wk 0, 2) | Integrin (moderate-severe CD; vs Placebo; GEMINI-2 wk 6 remission)

allOutcomes.title: Clinical remission (CDAI <=150) at wk 6 vedolizumab vs placebo (GEMINI-2 induction primary)

NCT01224171.snippet: Sandborn WJ et al. NEJM 2013;369:711-721 (GEMINI-2). Vedolizumab in CD. Remission 14.5% vs 6.8%.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Age 18 to 80~* Diagnosis of moderately to severely active Crohn's disease~* Crohn's Disease involvement of the ileum and/or colon~* Demonstrated, over the previous 5 year period, an inadequate response to, loss of response to, or intolerance of at least one conventional therapy as defined by the protocol~* May be receiving a therapeutic dose of conventional therapies for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) as defined by the protocol~Exclusion Criteria~* Evidence of abdominal ...

NCT01369329.name: UNITI-1
NCT01369329.group: Ustekinumab 6 mg/kg IV induction | IL-12/23 (moderate-severe CD, TNFi-failures; vs Placebo; UNITI-1 wk 8 response)

allOutcomes.title: Clinical response (CDAI decrease >=100) at wk 8 ustekinumab 6 mg/kg vs placebo (UNITI-1 primary; TNFi-failure population)

NCT01369329.snippet: Feagan BG et al. NEJM 2016;375:1946-1960 (UNITI-1 + UNITI-2). IL-12/23 class in CD; UNITI-1 enriched for TNFi-failures.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have Crohn's disease of at least 3 months' duration with colitis, ileitis, or ileocolitis, confirmed at some time in the past by radiography, histology, or endoscopy~* Have active Crohn's disease, defined as a baseline Crohn's Disease Activity Index (CDAI) score of >= 220 and <= 450~* Have received infliximab, adalimumab, or certolizumab pegol at a dose approved for the treatment of Crohn disease and did not respond initially (ie, primary nonresponse)~* Or responded init...

NCT03105128.name: ADVANCE
NCT03105128.group: Risankizumab 600 mg IV induction (wk 0, 4, 8) | IL-23p19 (moderate-severe CD; vs Placebo; ADVANCE wk 12 remission)

allOutcomes.title: CDAI clinical remission at wk 12 risankizumab 600 mg vs placebo (ADVANCE co-primary)

NCT03105128.snippet: D’Haens G et al. Lancet 2022;399:2015-2030 (ADVANCE + MOTIVATE). First IL-23p19-selective in CD. 45% vs 25% CDAI <150.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 16 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female aged >=18 to <= 80 years, or minimum age of adult consent according to local regulations, at the Baseline Visit. Where locally permissible, participants 16 to < 18 years of age who meet the definition of Tanner stage 5 for development at the Baseline Visit.~* Diagnosis of CD for at least 3 months prior to Baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderate to severe CD as assessed by stool frequency (SF), abdominal pain (AP) score, and Simple Endoscopic Score for...

NCT03345849.name: U-EXCEL
NCT03345849.group: Upadacitinib 45 mg PO daily x12 wk induction | Selective JAK1i (moderate-severe CD; vs Placebo; U-EXCEL wk 12 CDAI clinical remission, OR 4.69 from Loftus 2023 NEJM Table 2 - this OR represents the endoscopic-response co-primary; raw counts here are for CDAI clinical remission, the other co-primary).

allOutcomes.title: CDAI clinical remission at wk 12 upadacitinib 45 mg vs placebo (U-EXCEL primary; co-primary with endoscopic response)

NCT03345849.snippet: Loftus EV et al. NEJM 2023;388:1966-1980 (U-EXCEL + U-EXCEED). Highest induction-remission effect size among CD advanced therapies. 49% vs 19%.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f.
